cards.text: Submits application
cards.subtext: Online or in person

cards.text: Submission starts the regulatory clock
Optionally uploads supporting documents at submission
cards.subtext: Can also be uploaded any time during review

cards.text: Documents uploaded at submission are immediately available when the caseworker opens the task
Document-to-application linkage must be enforced at the UI layer
cards.subtext: The contract cannot prevent uploads directly to an external document system. The application screen must be the upload entry point to ensure documents link to the right verification item.

cards.text: Submits application on behalf of applicant
cards.subtext: Assisted or in-person submission

cards.text: Uploads documents provided by applicant at submission
Document-to-application linkage must be enforced at the UI layer
SNAP: 30-day processing clock starts
Expedited SNAP: 7-day clock
cards.subtext: If household meets expedited criteria — 7 CFR § 273.2(i)

cards.text: Medicaid: 45-day clock starts
cards.subtext: 90 days if disability determination needed — 42 CFR § 435.912

cards.text: application.submitted
cards.subtext: starts the regulatory clock; triggers caseworker task creation and confirmation notice

cards.text: Application created
cards.subtext: status: submitted, submittedAt, all submitted fields as structured data

cards.text: VerificationItems created
cards.subtext: Income, identity, and residency obligations — one record per requirement

cards.text: Workflow task created
cards.subtext: Added to intake queue; automated confirmation sent to applicant

cards.text: Future state: submission confirmation sent to applicant automatically
cards.subtext: Requires a communications contract — notification type, channel, and content are not yet defined.

cards.text: Application updated
cards.subtext: isExpedited: true, priority: expedited (if flagged)

cards.text: Future work: application.prescreened event
cards.subtext: Per-program eligibility indicators emitted by eligibility domain based on submitted (unverified) data — not a legal determination

cards.text: Federal checks initiated
cards.subtext: In response to application.submitted — FDSH, IEVS, SAVE, SSA, RTE (if Medicaid)

cards.text: Multi-program applications share check results across programs
cards.subtext: FDSH Hub consolidates access to SSA and DHS/SAVE data for Medicaid — running those checks separately alongside it is redundant. IEVS is the exception: it is a SNAP-specific federal mandate and runs regardless. Results should be shared across programs rather than re-run per program.

cards.text: Task created in intake queue
cards.subtext: In response to application.submitted — task routed to program-specific queue

cards.text: Person matching
cards.subtext: In response to application.submitted — links application to an existing person record. An exact match may surface a prior or active case. Fuzzy matches are flagged for caseworker review.

cards.text: Future work: client management domain contract
cards.subtext: Person matching results (exact, fuzzy, no match) and resolution require a client management contract defining the PersonMatchResult entity and person.match_* event types. Intake consumes the result but identity resolution is owned by client management.

cards.text: Queue routing and SLA assignment
cards.subtext: In response to application.submitted — routes to program-specific queue; assigns SLA track per program

cards.text: Future state: smart routing by caseworker skills
cards.subtext: Route expedited cases to experienced workers, disability-related Medicaid to specialists, and non-English applications to caseworkers with matching language skills. Requires caseload and skills visibility per worker.

cards.text: Expedited screening
cards.subtext: In response to application.submitted — evaluates submitted data against expedited criteria; publishes eligibility.application.expedited if applicable. Future work: requires eligibility domain contract.

cards.text: Application flagged as expedited
cards.subtext: In response to eligibility.application.expedited — updates application (isExpedited: true, assigns expedited SLA track)

cards.text: Future work: eligibility domain — program pre-screening
cards.subtext: Eligibility domain emits application.prescreened with per-program eligibility indicators. Informs queue routing and sets caseworker expectations — not a legal determination.

cards.text: Reviews prioritized queue and claims task
Opens application with pre-screened view
cards.subtext: Verification checklist reflects current status — items cleared by fast-returning federal checks or documents uploaded at submission are already resolved

cards.text: Queue shows SLA deadline and priority — caseworker sees what needs attention first

Open decision: sort order could be enforced server-side (API sort params) or client-side (UI sorts on slaInfo and priority fields). Data exists on each task; contract does not currently guarantee ordering.
Resolves fuzzy person match if flagged
cards.subtext: Reviews candidate person records and confirms the correct match — or creates a new record if none apply

cards.text: A matched person record may surface a prior or active case
cards.subtext: Caseworker should review any linked case history before proceeding — relevant circumstances may already be on file

cards.text: Future state: person match resolution requires client management domain contract — see #249
Schedules interview
Appointment-to-application linkage must be enforced at the UI layer
cards.subtext: The contract cannot prevent scheduling directly in an external tool. Launching the scheduler from the application screen ensures the appointment links back to the right application.

cards.text: Application updated
cards.subtext: status: under_review

cards.text: Interview record created
Interview record created
cards.subtext: In response to scheduling.appointment.scheduled — links appointment to application; creates interview record

cards.text: Future state: applicant receives notification when caseworker requests documents
cards.subtext: Triggered by caseworker action 'Requests additional documents for inconclusive items' — requires communications domain contract (#187)

cards.text: Uploads requested documents
Reviews verification checklist — acts on flagged and inconclusive items
Already-cleared items are shown resolved — caseworker sees only what needs attention
Confirms household data not resolvable electronically
cards.subtext: Demographics, household members, and relationships

cards.text: Only for items that couldn't clear through federal checks
Reviews discrepancies between submitted data and verification results
VerificationItem shows submitted value alongside check result — no switching systems to compare
Requests additional documents for inconclusive items
Future state: notification sent to applicant automatically
cards.subtext: Requires communications domain contract — see #187

cards.text: Unresolved items requiring applicant input carry to the interview
Future state: outreach tracked as a communication record
cards.subtext: Each document request recorded with what was asked, when, and by whom — visible on the application for audit and continuity.

cards.text: Uploads documents provided by applicant in person, by mail, or by fax
Document-to-application linkage must be enforced at the UI layer
Reviews documents uploaded by applicant
cards.subtext: Adjudicates each document against the verification item it satisfies; marks item resolved

cards.text: Escalates to supervisor when policy interpretation or override is needed
cards.subtext: Complex discrepancies, edge cases, or situations requiring judgment beyond standard guidance

cards.text: Reviews escalated case; provides guidance or takes over resolution
Guidance documented — creates an audit trail for the decision
Federal verifications required
cards.subtext: FDSH (citizenship), SAVE (immigration), IEVS (income, SNAP only), SSA (SSN), RTE (existing coverage, Medicaid only) — 42 U.S.C. § 1320b-7

cards.text: Medicaid: electronic verification before requesting paper documents
cards.subtext: Ex parte rule — electronic checks must be attempted first; paper documents can only be requested when checks return inconclusive — 42 CFR § 435.911

cards.text: VerificationItems updated
cards.subtext: Results from FDSH, IEVS, SAVE, SSA, RTE — status and result fields updated as results arrive

cards.text: DataExchangeRequests
cards.subtext: One per federal check; status updates as results return

cards.text: Verification checklist updated automatically as results arrive
cards.subtext: In response to incoming verification results — results push to the caseworker's view in real time; no refresh required

cards.text: VerificationItem status updated when document is adjudicated
Future work: eligibility domain re-evaluates indicators as material verification results arrive
cards.subtext: Pre-screening indicators from submission may be stale once IEVS, FDSH, or SSA results return. Eligibility domain re-runs screening on updated data and emits a new application.prescreened event — updated indicators surface to the caseworker in real time.

cards.text: Attends interview
cards.subtext: In person, by phone, or video depending on state policy

cards.text: Clarifies discrepancies flagged during verification review
cards.subtext: E.g. income reported differently than federal records — applicant explains or provides documentation

cards.text: Conducts SNAP interview
Resolves discrepancies requiring applicant input
cards.subtext: Updates record based on applicant clarification or documentation provided at interview

cards.text: Resolves remaining verification items
cards.subtext: Marks inconclusive items resolved or requests final documents

cards.text: Opportunity: Collect H.R.1 work requirement data at interview
cards.subtext: Stop-gap until the data model is updated to capture work requirement fields at submission. Supplemental interview questions cover hours worked per week, caregiver status and ages of dependents, and enrollment in approved work or community engagement programs. Caseworkers work from an H.R.1 supplement to the standard interview guide.

cards.text: Opportunity: Request work compliance documentation
cards.subtext: Use the existing document request mechanism for work requirement evidence — pay stubs, employer statements, volunteer logs, caregiving attestation, and medical exemption documentation. Flows through the standard caseworker document request → applicant upload path.

cards.text: SNAP: Interview required before determination
cards.subtext: May be waived by state policy — 7 CFR § 273.2(e)

cards.text: H.R.1: SNAP ABAWD work requirement expansion
cards.subtext: Expands age range subject to ABAWD work requirements; adds parental work requirements for adults with children above the age threshold. Existing application forms likely do not capture the data needed to evaluate compliance or exemption. — H.R.1 (pending)

cards.text: H.R.1: Medicaid community engagement requirement
cards.subtext: Adults required to demonstrate work, volunteering, caregiving, or education hours to maintain Medicaid eligibility. Data fields for engagement type and hours are not currently captured in most state application forms. — H.R.1 (pending)

cards.text: Interview updated
cards.subtext: Scheduled date, notes, and outcome recorded

cards.text: VerificationItems resolved
cards.subtext: Final status set for each obligation

cards.text: Future work: new VerificationItem types for H.R.1 compliance
cards.subtext: New obligation types needed — ABAWD work compliance, Medicaid community engagement, work requirement exemption. Each tracks submitted evidence and verification status alongside existing income and identity items.

cards.text: Reviews eligibility calculation results for unexpected outcomes
Exception review only — caseworker is not approving a calculation they triggered
Future state: decision support indicators
cards.subtext: System surfaces likely eligibility per program based on current record state — helps caseworker anticipate outcomes before the full rules engine runs.

cards.text: SNAP: Determination within 30 days (7 if expedited)
Medicaid: Determination within 45 days
application.review_completed
cards.subtext: Fires automatically when all VerificationItems are resolved — triggers eligibility domain, no caseworker action required

cards.text: Rules engine invoked automatically
cards.subtext: In response to application.review_completed — income and expense calculations against household data, per program

cards.text: Receives determination notice
cards.subtext: Approval, denial, or pending notice per program

cards.text: Records determination and rationale
cards.subtext: Eligibility outcome, date, and reasoning recorded per program

cards.text: Rationale supports appeals and quality review
Previews notice before it is sent
cards.subtext: Quality check — caseworker confirms notice content is accurate before it reaches the applicant

cards.text: Future state: notice preview generated by system
cards.subtext: System generates a draft notice for caseworker review before sending. Currently notices are queued automatically without a preview step.

cards.text: Approves determination
cards.subtext: Required by state policy

cards.text: Future state: supervisor approval queue
cards.subtext: Supervisor sees pending approvals in their own prioritized queue — not just a handoff from the caseworker. Requires supervisor-facing workflow tooling.

cards.text: Reviews sampled determinations for accuracy and regulatory compliance
QC review may be performed by supervisor or a dedicated quality reviewer
cards.subtext: Supports federal compliance audits — FNS for SNAP, CMS for Medicaid

cards.text: Future state: system-assisted QC sampling
cards.subtext: System automatically flags a statistically valid sample of closed cases for review — reviewer works from a QC queue rather than selecting cases manually

cards.text: Notice of Action required
cards.subtext: Adverse action notice required for denials and closures

cards.text: Application updated
cards.subtext: status: closed, disposition: approved | denied | referred

cards.text: Service case created only on approval
cards.subtext: A case record is created when an application is approved — not at submission. Linked to the matched or newly created person record.

cards.text: application.closed
cards.subtext: signals intake is complete; triggers case creation if approved

cards.text: Future state: notices generated and queued automatically
cards.subtext: Requires communications domain contract — see #187
